reasoning: In the short term (March/April 2025), ongoing debt ceiling dynamics are forcing the Treasury to draw down its General Account (TGA), pushing reserve balances upward, slightly overcoming the steady $60B/mo drain from QT. Therefore, the first two dates have a mildly positive probability of exceeding the March 16th baseline. By June, following a presumed debt limit resolution, the Treasury will issue debt to aggressively refill the TGA, simultaneously draining bank reserves. This, combined with three months of continued QT, strongly suggests reserves will drop substantially by summer. By late 2025 and early 2026, QT is expected to conclude, stabilizing reserve balances. However, higher uncertainty regarding overall macroeconomic factors (including the possibility of rate cuts, an early end to QT, or economic shocks requiring a policy pivot) brings the probabilities for distant horizon dates closer to 0.5.
